authoring_note: Between-point dead time following break-point situation. Player A off-camera, regathering. Shifted from t=0 to t=500ms per G20 to avoid collision with live FSM's initial transition at t=0.

Server enters frame, walking to baseline. The 7-state RallyMicroPhase taxonomy lacks a dedicated WALKING_TO_BASELINE phase (G31 deferred PlayerPosition stream to Phase B) - PRE_SERVE_RITUAL is the closest rally-micro phase aligned with 'approach and settle' per TenniSet literature.
Toss-motion initiation. Frame t_0011 shows mid-toss with ball in air at waist level — the toss BEGAN ~500-700ms earlier. t=10500ms is the visible onset of the serve motion (plant foot + loading arm).

Ball contact, point begins. Aligns with live FSM's own PRE_SERVE_RITUAL -> ACTIVE_RALLY transition at t=11200ms (reason=kinematic) - the authored timeline RE-CONVERGES to live CV here, which is the correct behavior (display-only authoring for the pre-live window, live CV takes over from the first real event).
